description: A candid outdoor photograph of the Frances Perkins Department of Labor Building in Washington, D.C. on a clear blue-sky day. The mid-century concrete federal building is decorated for a patriotic occasion: a very large American flag hangs vertically on the right facade, a tall flagpole flies the U.S. flag at half-staff in the center foreground, red-white-and-blue bunting decorates the lower level, and at left a large vertical banner bears a stylized portrait of President Donald Trump with text including 'AMERICA 250' and 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST.' Engraved on the building's entablature is 'FRANCES PERKINS DEPARTMENT OF LABOR BUILDING.' A tree and entrance steps are in the foreground. While the building-mounted banner is itself a designed propaganda piece, the posted image is a real photograph of the building rather than a standalone designed graphic/meme, so it is marked is_designed=false (candid documentary photo of an installation). The flag at half-staff and the somber tweet ('Our resolve is stronger than ever ... we will never give up our fight') suggest a memorial context (the post dates to Sept. 12, 2025). Messaging: patriotism and America-First worker advocacy.
notable_text: AMERICA 250 | AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST (on banner) | FRANCES PERKINS DEPARTMENT OF LABOR BUILDING (engraved)
